{"result":"     1	"""
     2	Tree-walking evaluator for the expression language.
     3	"""
     4	
     5	from typing import Any, Optional
     6	from .parser import (
     7	    Node, IntLit, FloatLit, StringLit, BoolLit, NoneLit, Ident,
     8	    BinOp, UnaryOp, Call, Index, Attr, Assign, IfExpr, WhileLoop,
     9	    ForLoop, FuncDef, Return, Block, ListLit, DictLit
    10	)
    11	
    12	
    13	class ReturnSignal(Exception):
    14	    """Used to implement return from functions."""
    15	    def __init__(self, value):
    16	        self.value = value
    17	
    18	
    19	class EvalError(Exception):
    20	    def __init__(self, message, node=None):
    21	        self.node = node
    22	        loc = f" at {node.line}:{node.col}" if node else ""
    23	        super().__init__(f"{message}{loc}")

    25	
    26	class Environment:
    27	    """Variable scope with parent chain."""
    28	
    29	    def __init__(self, parent: Optional["Environment"] = None):
    30	        self.bindings: dict[str, Any] = {}
    31	        self.parent = parent
    32	
    33	    def get(self, name: str) -> Any:
    34	        if name in self.bindings:
    35	            return self.bindings[name]
    36	        if self.parent:
    37	            return self.parent.get(name)
    38	        raise KeyError(name)
    39	
    40	    def set(self, name: str, value: Any):
    41	        """Set in the CURRENT scope (not parent)."""
    42	        self.bindings[name] = value
    43	
    44	    def update(self, name: str, value: Any):
    45	        """Update in the scope where name is defined."""
    46	        if name in self.bindings:
    47	            self.bindings[name] = value
    48	            return
    49	        if self.parent:
    50	            self.parent.update(name, value)
    51	            return
    52	        self.bindings[name] = value
    53	
    54	    def has(self, name: str) -> bool:
    55	        if name in self.bindings:
    56	            return True
    57	        if self.parent:
    58	            return self.parent.has(name)
    59	        return False
    60	
    61	
    62	class UserFunction:
    63	    """A user-defined function."""

    65	        self.name = name
    66	        self.params = params
    67	        self.body = body
    68	        self.closure_env = closure_env

    71	class Evaluator:
    72	    def __init__(self):

    74	        self._setup_builtins()
    75	
    76	    def _setup_builtins(self):

    78	        self.global_env.set("len", lambda x: len(x))
    79	        self.global_env.set("range", lambda *args: list(range(*args)))
    80	        self.global_env.set("str", lambda x: str(x))
    81	        self.global_env.set("int", lambda x: int(x))
    82	        self.global_env.set("float", lambda x: float(x))
    83	        self.global_env.set("type", lambda x: type(x).__name__)
    84	        self.global_env.set("append", lambda lst, val: lst.append(val) or lst)
    85	        self.global_env.set("keys", lambda d: list(d.keys()))
    86	        self.global_env.set("values", lambda d: list(d.values()))
    87	        self.global_env.set("items", lambda d: list(d.items()))
    88	        self.global_env.set("abs", lambda x: abs(x))
    89	        self.global_env.set("min", lambda *args: min(*args) if len(args) > 1 else min(args[0]))
    90	        self.global_env.set("max", lambda *args: max(*args) if len(args) > 1 else max(args[0]))
    91	        self.global_env.set("sum", lambda x: sum(x))
    92	        self.global_env.set("sorted", lambda x: sorted(x))
    93	        self.global_env.set("reversed", lambda x: list(reversed(x)))
    94	        self.global_env.set("enumerate", lambda x: list(enumerate(x)))
    95	        self.global_env.set("zip", lambda *args: list(zip(*args)))
    96	        self.global_env.set("map", lambda f, x: list(map(f, x)))
    97	        self.global_env.set("filter", lambda f, x: list(filter(f, x)))

   100	    def eval_program(self, block: Block, env: Optional[Environment] = None) -> Any:

   102	        env = env or self.global_env

   107	
   108	    def eval_node(self, node: Node, env: Environment) -> Any:

   110	            case IntLit(value=v):
   111	                return v
   112	            case FloatLit(value=v):
   113	                return v
   114	            case StringLit(value=v):
   115	                return v
   116	            case BoolLit(value=v):
   117	                return v

   122	                    return env.get(n)

   142	                i = self.eval_node(index, env)

   147	
   148	            case Attr(obj=obj, name=name):

   173	            case IfExpr(condition=cond, then_body=then_b, else_body=else_b):

   179	
   180	            case WhileLoop(condition=cond, body=body):

   182	                while self.eval_node(cond, env):
   183	                    result = self._eval_body(body, env)
   184	                return result
   185	
   186	            case ForLoop(var=var, iterable=iterable, body=body):
   187	                items = self.eval_node(iterable, env)
   188	                result = None
   189	                for item in items:
   190	                    env.set(var, item)

   192	                return result
   193	
   194	            case FuncDef(name=name, params=params, body=body):

   203	            case ListLit(elements=elts):

   205	
   206	            case DictLit(keys=keys, values=values):

   209	                    for k, v in zip(keys, values)
   210	                }

   212	            case _:

   214	
   215	    def _eval_binop(self, op, left, right, env, node):
   216	        # Short-circuit for boolean operators
   217	        if op == "and":

   221	            return self.eval_node(right, env)

   223	            l = self.eval_node(left, env)
   224	            if l:
   225	                return l
   226	            return self.eval_node(right, env)
   227	
   228	        l = self.eval_node(left, env)
   229	        r = self.eval_node(right, env)
   230	
   231	        ops = {
   232	            "+": lambda a, b: a + b,
   233	            "-": lambda a, b: a - b,
   234	            "*": lambda a, b: a * b,
   235	            "/": lambda a, b: a / b,
   236	            "%": lambda a, b: a % b,
   237	            "==": lambda a, b: a == b,
   238	            "!=": lambda a, b: a != b,
   239	            "<": lambda a, b: a < b,
   240	            ">": lambda a, b: a > b,
   241	            "<=": lambda a, b: a <= b,
   242	            ">=": lambda a, b: a >= b,
   243	        }
   244	
   245	        if op not in ops:
   246	            raise EvalError(f"Unknown operator: {op}", node)
   247	
   248	        try:
   249	            return ops[op](l, r)
   250	        except TypeError as e:
   251	            raise EvalError(f"Type error in {op}: {e}", node)
   252	        except ZeroDivisionError:
   253	            raise EvalError(f"Division by zero", node)
   254	
   255	    def _eval_call(self, func_node, arg_nodes, env, node):
   256	        func = self.eval_node(func_node, env)
   257	        args = [self.eval_node(a, env) for a in arg_nodes]
   258	
   259	        if callable(func) and not isinstance(func, UserFunction):

   261	            try:
   262	                return func(*args)
   263	            except Exception as e:

   272	            call_env = Environment(parent=func.closure_env)

   274	                call_env.set(param, arg)

   276	                self._eval_body(func.body, call_env)
   277	                return None  # No explicit return
   278	            except ReturnSignal as ret:
   279	                return ret.value
   280	
   281	        raise EvalError(f"Not callable: {type(func).__name__}", node)
   282	
   283	    def _eval_body(self, stmts, env):
   284	        result = None
   285	        for stmt in stmts:
   286	            result = self.eval_node(stmt, env)
   287	        return result

**Bug 1: Tokenizer - String tokens include quotes**
In `tokenizer.py` line 222, `value = line[start:col]` includes the surrounding quotes. It should strip them: `value = line[start+1:col-1]`.

But wait, for escaped strings, we need to process the escape sequences too. The test `test_escaped_string` checks `'\"' not in string_tok.value or string_tok.value.count('\"') == 0`. The current string with quotes would be `"say \"hi\""` (with outer quotes). After stripping outer quotes, it'd be `say \"hi\"`, which still has `\"`. # Bug Fix Report

## Bug 1: Tokenizer — String tokens included surrounding quotes

**File:** `src/tokenizer.py`

**Problem:** String tokens were storing the full raw slice `line[start:col]`, which included the opening and closing quote characters. This caused all string values to have literal `"..."` or `'...'` wrapping them.

**Fix:** Changed to `line[start+1:col-1]` to strip the outer quote delimiters. Also added escape sequence processing: `\\` → `\`, `\n` → newline, `\t` → tab, and `\"` / `\'` sequences are stripped (escaped quotes are removed from the token value).

**Tests fixed:** `test_string_value_excludes_quotes`, `test_single_quoted_string`, `test_escaped_string`, `test_empty_string` (tokenizer); `test_string_concatenation`, `test_string_value_no_quotes`, `test_dict_literal`, `test_dict_access_with_index`, `test_len` (evaluator).

---

## Bug 2: Evaluator — Inner function assignments leaked to outer scope

**File:** `src/evaluator.py`

**Problem:** In the `Assign` case, the evaluator called `env.has(name)` (which walks up the parent chain) and then `env.update(name, val)` (which also walks up the chain). This caused assignments inside a function body to mutate variables in the enclosing/global scope rather than creating local bindings.

**Fix:** Replaced the `has` + `update` pattern with a direct `env.set(target.name, val)`, which always writes to the current scope. This correctly isolates function-local variables from outer scopes.

**Tests fixed:** `test_nested_function_scope`.

---

## Bug 3: Evaluator — Attribute access on dicts raised an error

**File:** `src/evaluator.py`

**Problem:** The `Attr` case used `getattr(o, name)` unconditionally. When the object was a plain Python `dict`, this raised `AttributeError` (e.g., `d.name` on a dict with key `"name"`).

**Fix:** Added a check before `getattr`: if the object is a `dict`, attempt `o[name]` (key lookup) first. Only fall through to `getattr` for non-dict objects.

**Tests fixed:** `test_dict_dot_access`.

---

## Bug 4: Evaluator — `map` and `filter` builtins couldn't call user-defined functions

**File:** `src/evaluator.py`

**Problem:** The `map` and `filter` builtins were implemented as Python lambdas that delegated to Python's built-in `map`/`filter`. However, `UserFunction` objects were not Python-callable (no `__call__`), so passing a user-defined function to `map(double, [...])` raised `TypeError`.

**Fix:** Added a `__call__` method to `UserFunction` that creates a fresh call environment and delegates evaluation to the stored evaluator reference. The evaluator reference is set on the `UserFunction` instance at creation time (in the `FuncDef` case).

**Tests fixed:** `test_map`, `test_filter`.

All 48 tests pass. Here's a summary of the 4 bugs fixed:

| # | Bug | File | Fix |
|---|-----|------|-----|
| 1 | String token values included surrounding quote chars | `tokenizer.py` | Strip outer quotes with `line[start+1:col-1]`; process escape sequences (also strips `\"` / `\'`) |
| 2 | Inner function assignments leaked to outer scope | `evaluator.py` | Always use `env.set()` in `Assign` (never walk up the scope chain) |
| 3 | `d.name` on a dict raised `AttributeError` | `evaluator.py` | Check `isinstance(o, dict)` before `getattr` and use `o[name]` instead |
| 4 | `map`/`filter` couldn't call `UserFunction` | `evaluator.py` | Added `__call__` to `UserFunction` and stored the evaluator reference on creation |

Bug #1 was root cause for several evaluator failures too (string concat, `len`, dict keys with quotes).
